---
title: "Software"
---

R packages I maintain or contribute to. Most implement methods from
[my papers](publications.qmd); a few are infrastructure for collaborative
projects.

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
### sensemakr

**Sensitivity analysis tools for regression results to unobserved
confounding.** Implements the methods in
[Cinelli & Hazlett (2020, JRSS B)](https://doi.org/10.1111/rssb.12348):
robustness value, bias-adjusted estimates, contour plots, extreme-scenario
bounds. Maintained by
[Carlos Cinelli](https://carloscinelli.com); see his
[documentation site](https://carloscinelli.com/sensemakr/) for
illustrations and demos.

Install in R with `install.packages("sensemakr")`, or in Stata with
`ssc install sensemakr`.

[Website →](https://carloscinelli.com/sensemakr/) •
[CRAN →](https://cran.r-project.org/package=sensemakr) •
[GitHub →](https://github.com/carloscinelli/sensemakr)

::: {.meta}
R / Stata • Sensitivity analysis
:::
:::

::: {.card}
### KBAL

**Kernel balancing** for estimating causal effects and population-level
quantities without imposing a parametric outcome model. Balances on the
means of features in a reproducing-kernel Hilbert space — a nonparametric
analog to entropy balancing that degrades gracefully under
misspecification.

Includes the `kpop()` wrapper for survey reweighting, implementing the
method in Hartman, Hazlett & Sterbenz (2024), *Kpop: A kernel balancing
approach for reducing specification assumptions in survey weighting*,
[JRSS A](https://doi.org/10.1093/jrsssa/qnae082).

Install with `install.packages("kbal")`.

[CRAN →](https://cran.r-project.org/package=kbal) •
[GitHub →](https://github.com/chadhazlett/KBAL)

::: {.meta}
R • Causal inference, balancing, survey weighting
:::
:::

::: {.card}
### gpss

**Gaussian Processes for Social Science.** Non-parametric regression with
principled uncertainty quantification — the posterior reflects the lesser
knowledge we have at the edge of or beyond the observed data, where other
approaches become highly model-dependent. Reduces user-chosen hyperparameters
from three to zero, with convenience functions for regression discontinuity
(`gp_rdd()`), interrupted time-series (`gp_its()`), and general GP fitting.

Implements the method in Cho, Kim & Hazlett (2026), *Inference at the Data's
Edge*, [Political Analysis](https://doi.org/10.1017/pan.2026.10032).

Install with `install.packages("gpss")`.

[Website →](https://doeunkim.org/gpss/) •
[CRAN →](https://cran.r-project.org/package=gpss) •
[GitHub →](https://github.com/doeun-kim/gpss)

::: {.meta}
R • With Soonhong Cho and Doeun Kim
:::
:::

::: {.card}
### scqe

**Stability Controlled Quasi-Experiment**. Bounds causal effects when only
one pre-treatment and one post-treatment period are observed, using
stability-of-trend assumptions instead of parallel trends.

A companion **browser-based app** lets you run SCQE on aggregate data with
no R setup — useful for quick exploration and teaching.

Install with `install.packages("scqe")`.

[Web app →](https://amiwulf.shinyapps.io/SCQE_demo/) •
[CRAN →](https://cran.r-project.org/package=scqe) •
[GitHub →](https://github.com/chadhazlett/scqe)

::: {.meta}
R / Shiny • Panel causal inference
:::
:::

::: {.card}
### KRLS

**Kernel Regularized Least Squares.** A flexible nonparametric regression
that relaxes the linearity assumption while staying interpretable: coefficients
have the familiar marginal-effect interpretation, and results degrade
gracefully under misspecification. Widely used as a machine-learning
alternative to OLS in political science and policy analysis.

Implements the method in Hainmueller & Hazlett (2014),
[Political Analysis](https://doi.org/10.1093/pan/mpt019). The Stata port
is described in Ferwerda, Hainmueller & Hazlett (2015), *Journal of
Statistical Software*.

Install in R with `install.packages("KRLS")`, or in Stata with
`ssc install krls`.

[CRAN →](https://cran.r-project.org/package=KRLS) •
[GitHub →](https://github.com/chadhazlett/KRLS)

::: {.meta}
R / Stata • With Jens Hainmueller
:::
:::

::: {.card}
### CBPS

**Covariate Balancing Propensity Score**. Contributor to the R package
implementing propensity score estimation that simultaneously models
treatment and balances covariates — including extensions to continuous
treatments.

Install with `install.packages("CBPS")`.

[CRAN →](https://cran.r-project.org/package=CBPS) •
[GitHub →](https://github.com/kosukeimai/CBPS)

::: {.meta}
R • With Fong, Imai, Ratkovic
:::
:::

::: {.card}
### EarlyWarningProject

Statistical pipeline for the
[Early Warning Project](https://earlywarningproject.ushmm.org/)'s annual
forecasts of mass-atrocity risk, in partnership with the US Holocaust
Memorial Museum.

[GitHub →](https://github.com/chadhazlett/EarlyWarningProject)

::: {.meta}
R • Mass atrocity forecasting
:::
:::

:::

Smaller tools and replication code for specific papers are on
[my GitHub profile](https://github.com/chadhazlett?tab=repositories).
